\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\begin{threeparttable}
\caption{Diagnostic decompositions of the TWFE estimator (staggered articles)}
\label{tab:bacon_summary}
\small
\begin{tabular}{lc}
\toprule
\textbf{Statistic} & \textbf{Value} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Bacon decomposition ($N = 26$)}} \\
\quad Mean treated-vs-treated share & 0.349 \\
\quad Articles with TvT $> 50\%$ & 8 / 26 (31\%) \\[6pt]
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{de Chaisemartin--d'Haultfoeuille weights ($N = 35$)}} \\
\quad Articles with any negative weight & 25 / 35 (71\%) \\
\quad Mean share of negative weights & 0.064 \\
\quad Median share of negative weights & 0.004 \\
\quad Maximum share of negative weights & 0.485 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item \textit{Notes}: The Bacon decomposition \citep{goodman2021difference} partitions the TWFE estimator into $2\times2$ comparisons; treated-vs-treated (TvT) includes ``Earlier vs Later'' and ``Later vs Earlier'' comparisons, where already-treated units serve as controls. 4 articles have TvT $= 100\%$, meaning all units are eventually treated and the entire TWFE estimate relies on timing variation. The de Chaisemartin--d'Haultfoeuille decomposition \citep{de2020two} expresses TWFE as a weighted sum of group--time ATTs; share of negative weights $= |\Sigma\,\omega^{-}| \,/\, (|\Sigma\,\omega^{-}| + \Sigma\,\omega^{+})$. 3 staggered articles are excluded from the Bacon computation due to panel size exceeding computational limits or lack of treatment variation in the balanced panel.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
